episode_title: Mattia Peretti: Balancing AI Innovation with Journalistic Integrity
transcript_file: 2024-05-01 - Mattia Peretti Balancing AI Innovation with Journalistic Integrity.txt
summary_claims: The news industry has moved past the peak generative AI hype and into a more pragmatic phase at the beginning of 2024, with organizations focusing on strategy and real value rather than trying everything
Automated article summaries increase time on page rather than replacing full article reads, because they serve as entry points that get people interested in reading more

Challenges explored with AI in journalism in 2020 (AI-powered summaries, unlocking news archives) are still being explored today, showing these are ongoing evolving problems rather than ones with a single solution
AI literacy, experimentation/products, and guidelines/principles form a triangle where none can succeed without the other two

matches.theme_name: The Automation Paradox: What AI Replaces, What Remains Irreplaceable

best_quote.text: Schibsted and many others have had quite a success in just introducing automated summaries to their articles... seeing that time on page kind of like increases as well. So it's not that we give people a summary and they just read that and ignore the rest.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti describes AI summaries as entry points that increase engagement rather than replace articles, and notes that investigative AI for dataset analysis has been a proven use case for years, while human-in-the-loop remains essential for editorial quality.
matches.matched_text: This is Newsroom Robots, the podcast where we explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and the news industry. I'm Nikita Roy, data scientist, media entrepreneur, and one of the many founders currently building their. Ventures at the Harvard Innovation Labs. On the Newsroom Robots, I'm excited to bring you insightful conversations with industry experts about how AI is impacting the way we do journalism. Joining me today is Mattia Peretti, a Knight Fellow with the International Center for Journalists. Mattia's current work focuses on exploring innovative solutions. to create sustainable paths forward for journalism. Before his fellowship, Matias served as the manager of Journalism AI A joint initiative between POLIS, the Journalism Think Tank at the London School of Economics, and the Google News Initiative.

matches.theme_name: Trust on the Line: Credibility, Transparency, and the Audience Contract

best_quote.text: We need to do much more work in building that relationship and being independent from any tool, from any platform. We have seen it with social media putting down in their priorities news and not producing it anymore.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti emphasizes building direct audience relationships independent of platforms as crucial for trust preservation, noting that past dependence on social media platforms should serve as a warning for how newsrooms approach AI platforms.
matches.matched_text: we need to do much more work in building that relationship and being independent from any tool, from any platform. We have seen it, you know, with tech com the social media, you know, putting down in their priorities news and so not producing it anymore.

matches.theme_name: The Ethics Infrastructure: From Principles on Paper to Practice in the Newsroom

best_quote.text: I feel like the risk with putting too strict guidelines is to communicate to your team that you don't trust them to know how to do the right thing. To really send the message that we as the management team or this AI working group, we are really afraid that you're gonna mess it up.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti draws on his Guardian experience to argue forcefully for principles over prescriptive guidelines, noting that overly strict rules become outdated within a month and risk communicating distrust, while guidelines without literacy and experimentation become 'just a performative exercise.'
matches.matched_text: I feel like they are part of funding a lot of the journalism work that is going on with tech right now. Yeah. I think the relationship with tech companies is honestly very, very tricky. And they've been uh uh you know adapting and updating my vision and my point of view on this over over the years because I understand the arguments on all sides to be honest and I find it harder to take a position myself because Inevitably i there are players we need to interface ourselves with.

matches.theme_name: Building the Machine: Technical Architecture and Engineering Decisions

best_quote.text: The limitations of large language models are still significant and need to be considered when repackaging content, despite improvements.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Technical architecture receives minimal attention; Peretti acknowledges LLM limitations but focuses on strategic and organizational questions rather than engineering details.
matches.matched_text: the limitations of large language models are still significant and needs to be considered. And then in general, of course, if we look beyond the generative AI hype and the tools of the last uh, I don't know, what are we now?

matches.theme_name: The Literacy Imperative: Upskilling the Newsroom for an AI Era

best_quote.text: There is the AI literacy, there are the experiments in building products, and then everyone has been rushing to come up with guidelines and principles for generative AI and so on. Neither of these three things can exist and can have success without the other two, in my opinion.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti introduces the 'triangle' model where AI literacy, experimentation, and guidelines must develop in parallel, drawing on his Internews experience facilitating knowledge-sharing communities across 100+ countries and arguing that not everyone needs to become an AI expert but everyone needs basic literacy.
matches.matched_text: There is the AI literacy, there are the experiments in building products, and then everyone has been rushing to come up with guidelines and principles for generative AI and so on. Neither of these three things can exist and can have success without the other two, in my opinion.

matches.theme_name: Organizing for AI: Leadership, Strategy, and Institutional Transformation

best_quote.text: I wish that sometimes there was more time spent on a step before thinking, okay, what are we trying to do? What are our goals in the first place? And is AI in this case actually helping us can help us achieve that goal rather than approaching it immediately as we need to use it.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti challenges the assumption that AI adoption is inherently valuable, advocating for 'why before how' thinking, arguing against scaling as a goal in itself, and describing the post-hype pragmatic phase where organizations are consolidating toward genuine value rather than trying everything.
matches.matched_text: I wish that sometimes there was more time spent on a step before thinking, okay, what are we trying to do? What are our goals in the first place? And is AI in this case actually Helping us can help us achieve that goal rather than approaching it immediately as we need to use it.

matches.theme_name: Power, Money, and the Platform: Big Tech and the News Industry

best_quote.text: I think it's just very, very important that we're not naive about what are their goals. At the end of the day these are for profit organizations. Their mission is profit. The mission is not the mission that we have as journalists.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti articulates the tech-publisher tension with unusual candor, acknowledging his own Google funding while arguing tech companies should pay for news data, describing the relationship as 'very, very tricky,' and warning against the naivety of treating profit-driven companies as mission-aligned partners.
matches.matched_text: I think it's just very, very important that we're not naive about what our their goals. At the end of the day is these are for profit organizations. Their mission is profit. The mission is not the mission that we have as journalists.

matches.theme_name: Reimagining the News Product: Personalization, Formats, and the Experience Layer

best_quote.text: What Artifact was doing beyond the personalization of content, the possibilities to repackage a piece of information in different formats for different audiences was something that I was really enjoying as a user.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti highlights Artifact's content repackaging as a model and notes the Schibsted summary success, positioning format flexibility and personalization as areas where AI creates genuine user value.
matches.matched_text: what Artifact was doing beyond the personalization of content, the possibilities that to uh repackage a piece of information in different formats for different audiences was something that I was really enjoying as a user playing around with and so I know that ChipsD and many others have had quite a success in just introducing uh automated summaries.

matches.theme_name: Survival Economics: Business Models, Revenue, and Market Position in the AI Era

best_quote.text: These tech companies are the richest organizations and companies on the planet. So it's not that, oh my god, if we don't pay you for our content, we will just fail. That's just not the case.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Economic concerns surface through the platform dependency discussion and the argument that tech companies can afford to pay for news content, but business models are not a sustained focus.
matches.matched_text: These tech companies are the richest organizations and companies on the planet. So it's not that, oh my god, if we don't pay you for our con for your content, we will just fail. That's Just not the case.

matches.theme_name: Regulating the Revolution: Law, Policy, and Journalism as Accountability

best_quote.text: We have seen it with social media putting down in their priorities news and so not producing it anymore. Or we have seen over the last ten, fifteen years social media becoming super relevant for a little bit and then disappearing, regulation changing.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Regulation is mentioned only in passing as part of the broader platform dependency discussion, without detailed engagement with specific regulatory frameworks.
matches.matched_text: We have seen it, you know, with tech com the social media, you know, putting down in their priorities news and so not producing it anymore. Or we have seen, you know, over the last ten, fifteen years social media becoming super relevant for a little bit and then disappearing, regulation changing.

matches.theme_name: The Prototype Pipeline: From Experiment to Shipped Product

best_quote.text: Challenges explored with AI in journalism in 2020 are still being explored today, showing these are ongoing evolving problems rather than ones with a single solution.
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti notes the persistence of the same AI challenges from 2020 to 2024, implicitly acknowledging the production gap, but the episode does not focus on the mechanics of moving prototypes to production.

matches.theme_name: Data Privacy, Source Protection, and the Boundaries of Responsible Use

best_quote.text: We're gaining efficiency, we're doing some things faster. What are we losing? Like I use sometimes ChatGPT for helping me brainstorm an idea... But I also worry if I rely too much on it, what muscle in my brain I'm not exercising anymore?
best_quote.speaker: Mattia Peretti

matches.summary: Peretti raises the novel concern of cognitive atrophy from AI dependency -- a personal data/privacy consideration about what we lose when we delegate cognitive functions to external tools.
matches.matched_text: we're gaining efficiency, we're doing some things faster. What are we what are we losing? Like I use sometimes, that's I don't know, some I use case I use ChatGPT for helping me brainstorm an idea or start something new, and it is helpful sometimes.
